title: U54 SCENT Histology H&E Staining
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"329g","text":"To provide a standardized method of running, cleaning, refilling, and verifying quality of the automatic H\u0026E stainer. Every morning before running slides for projects, a quality control slide containing brain, kidney, liver, and pancreas tissue is run, reviewed, and deemed acceptable by a histotechnician, who documents this review.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Open cover of autostainer and remove each lid of the reagents inside. Stack lids and set to the right side of autostainer. Close cover.
Hit switch on the right side of autostainer to turn on and initialize.
Once initialization is complete, press button on screen to select staining program 1.
Take one slide from the box of unstained H&E control slides and label with: a. H&E QA b. Date c. Tech initials
Place the slide into a black 30-count slide rack.
Pull the lever below the loading drawer on the autostainer to open.
Place slide rack inside and close.
Hit button to the left of loading drawer to begin staining run.
When staining has finished, autostainer will periodically beep.
Pull the lever below the exit drawer on the autostainer to open.
Press button next to exit drawer so instrument knows the rack was removed. (Autostainer will continue to beep otherwise.)
Coverslip and microscopically verify correct staining of control tissues.
Denote onRHL Internal H&E Histologic Quality Check. If staining quality is suboptimal, troubleshoot.
If staining is acceptable,write “PASS” on slide andstore in labeled control slide box next to autostainer.
File form in H&E quality control binder at the end of the month.
Any research slides for the day can be loaded in the same way once the review is complete.
At the end of the workday, open cover and replace reagent lids to prevent evaporation. Close cover.

Autostainer Program 1 Station # Solution Time 1 1 Xylene 2 min 2 2 Xylene 2 min 3 3 Xylene 2 min 4 4 100% Alcohol 2 min 5 5 100% Alcohol 30 sec 6 6 95% Alcohol 2 min* 7 7 95% Alcohol 1 min 8 Wash 5 Water wash 1 min 9 8 Hematoxylin* 30 sec* 10 Wash 4 Water Wash 30 sec 11 Wash 3 Water Wash 2 min 12 9 Clearing Solution 30 sec 13 Wash 2 Water Wash 1 min 14 10 Bluing Solution 30 sec 15 Wash 1 Water Wash 30 sec 16 11 80% Alcohol 30 sec 17 12 Eosin* 30 sec 18 13 95% Alcohol 30 sec 19 14 95% Alcohol 30 sec 20 15 100% Alcohol 30 sec 21 16 100% Alcohol 30 sec 22 17 Xylene 30 sec 23 18 Xylene 30 sec 24 Exit (19) Xylene Hold-exit station *These are the only two stations with hard times. Slides may be held longer in other stations when multiple racks are being run at the same time.
At the end of the work week, remove all staining containers and empty into the chemical waste containers located below autostainer
Rinse containers out with water and, working over the sink, fill with a 10% bleach solution for at least 15 minutes or until visibly clean.
Rinse out containers and place upright on a counter that has been covered with Wypall pads. Leave to air dry over the weekend.
Fill water wash containers with 10% bleach and then 3 changes of waterto remove bleach residue (containers will automatically drain when filled).

Flush 10% bleach solution followed with 3 changes of water through the base of the stainer. Use a bleach-soaked Wypall pad to spot clean any splashed staining reagents. NOTE: Manual H&E stain line should remain set up until autostainer is refilled and tested the next week to ensure one staining line is always available for use.
At the beginning of the work week, refill staining containers and place back into autostainer.

Reagent setup is as follows: A B C D E F G H I J K L 1 Xylene Xylene Xylene 100% Alcohol 100% Alcohol 95% Alcohol 95% Alcohol Wash 1 Wash 2 Wash 3 Wash 4 Wash 5 2 Xylene Xylene Xylene 100% Alcohol 100% Alcohol 95% Alcohol 95% Alcohol Eosin 80% Alcohol Bluing Solution Clearing Solution Harris Hematoxylin
Mark reagents changed on Autostainer Maintenance Log.
creator: Valerie Bekker
